LAW AND MOTION 302, 3. PLAINTIFF STEPHEN MALLOY'S MOTION FOR STATEMENT OF DECISION.PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FILED DECEMBER 16, 2022 (LARGELY DUPLICATIVE OF HIS DECEMBER 12 AND 15 MOTIONS) IS DENIED.A RECONSIDERATION MOTION MUST BE "BASED UPON NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW" AND A VALID REASON FOR NOT PRESENTING THEM EARLIER. (CCP SEC. 1008; GILBERD V. AC TRANSIT (1995) 32 CAL.APP.4TH 1494, 1500.) THE MOVING PARTY SHALL STATE BY AFFIDAVIT "WHAT NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW ARE CLAIMED TO BE SHOWN." (CCP SEC. 1008.) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THESE LAWS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STEPHEN MALLOY'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FILED DECEMBER 15, 2022 (LARGELY DUPLICATIVE OF HIS DECEMBER 12 MOTION) IS DENIED. A RECONSIDERATION MOTION MUST BE "BASED UPON NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW" AND A VALID REASON FOR NOT PRESENTING THEM EARLIER. (CCP SEC. 1008; GILBERD V. AC TRANSIT (1995) 32 CAL.APP.4TH 1494, 1500.) THE MOVING PARTY SHALL STATE BY AFFIDAVIT "WHAT NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES, OR LAW ARE CLAIMED TO BE SHOWN." (CCP SEC. 1008.) PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FAILS TO COMPLY WITH THESE LAWS. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Motion For Issuance Writ Of Mandate Per Ccp 1085, Ccp 1094.5, Ccp 425.10 Complaint; Ccp 527.6 Emotional Distress
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER STEPHEN MALLOY's Motion For Issuance Writ Of Mandate Per Ccp 1085, Ccp 1094.5, Ccp 425.10 Complaint; Ccp 527.6 Emotional Distress.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's petition for writ of mandate is denied.

In violation of California Rules of Court Rule 3.1112(a)(3), plaintiff fails to provide a memorandum of points and authorities that explains why he is entitled to the requested relief. The record demonstrates that: (1) plaintiff made a broad request for "Everything of Public Record concerning Stephen Malloy in any Department of Public Health Department"; (2) defendant sought clarification regarding the request; (3) plaintiff failed to respond; and (4) defendant closed the request. (Lim Decl., Ex. A.)

Petitioner fails to demonstrate an abuse of discretion or entitlement to traditional or administrative mandate. "Unquestionably, public records must be described clearly enough to permit the agency to determine whether writings of the type described in the request are under its control." (California First Amendment Coalition v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 159, 165.) "Petitioner should have presented a specific and focused request to the City, with which it then would have an opportunity to comply. It makes no sense to permit an individual to make a general, unfocused request for records to the public agency which will then be compelled to deny it, thereby ensuring litigation. The request to the agency must itself be focused and specific." (Rogers v. Superior Court (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 469, 481.)

Defendant correctly notes that there is no pending records request and at this point, plaintiff should submit a new and more focused request. The court further notes that plaintiff does not explain a basis to recover damages. "[M]andamus is generally said not to be an appropriate remedy for recovering money." (Wenzler v. Municipal Court of Pasadena Judicial Dist. (1965) 235 Cal.App.2d 128, 133.)

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion In Conformance For Reconsideration Ccp 1008, Move
To Strike Ccp 436; And Order To Show Cause Ccp 128. 7 & Crc 2.30. Re: Application For Writ Of Mandamus Ccp 1094.5 Complaint Ccp 425.10
Off Calendar Jan-12-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1. PETITIONER STEPHEN MALLOY'S Motion In Conformance For Reconsideration Ccp 1008, Move
To Strike Ccp 436; And Order To Show Cause Ccp 128. 7 & Crc 2.30. Re: Application For Writ Of Mandamus Ccp 1094.5 Complaint Ccp 425.10.

Plaintiff's motion filed December 12, 2022 is denied.

A reconsideration motion must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP Sec. 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) The moving party shall state by affidavit "what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (CCP Sec. 1008.) Plaintiff's motion fails to comply with these laws.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)
